title: external-agents run record
description: Contract for the per-run records scripts/run-agent.sh emits: the per-run meta.json record (write_meta_json) and the index.jsonl row (append_index_row = the record augmented with run_id/project). Control-plane facts only — never transcript text or the prompt. Evolution is additive-only: existing fields are never renamed, retyped, or dropped without a major version bump; new fields may be added (see docs/run-record-contract.md).

oneOf.title: meta.json record
oneOf.description: One per-run metadata record (no run_id / project).

oneOf.title: index.jsonl row
oneOf.description: One run-index row: the record augmented with the fan-out run_id and the project namespace.

run_id.description: Groups every agent of one fan-out under a single id.

project.description: The project namespace the run was recorded under.

record.description: The per-run metadata record. Each value is the post-fallback resolved truth (what was actually used), built only from values resolved at launch/collect — never parsed from the transcript.

agent.description: The agent name (agy/codex/claude/cursor, or a registered agent).

model.description: The resolved model actually used (post-fallback for agy).

effort.description: The tier's native effort, or '(none)' if unset.

target.description: The resolved directory the agent worked in.

rc.description: The agent process exit code (number; raw string only if non-numeric).

sec.description: Wall-clock seconds (number; raw string only if non-numeric).

bytes.description: Redacted transcript size in bytes (number; raw string only if non-numeric).

fallback.description: true iff the agy quota fallback swapped the primary model.

error_class.description: Phase 8.2 closed-set outcome class. Additive/optional (absent on records emitted before it existed).

signals.description: Best-effort, CLI-self-reported cost/usage signals — NOT billing-grade. The literal string 'unavailable' means no signal was recognized (never a fabricated number).

tokens.description: A numeric token count, or the literal string 'unavailable'.

cost.description: The cost string kept verbatim (e.g. '$0.12'), or the literal string 'unavailable'.
